title: Keep selected article visible when filtered out by unread filter
description: When user clicks an article in the unread list, it gets marked read and vanishes from the list on refetch because is_read=false filter excludes it. User wants selected article pinned in list until selection changes. Fix: track sticky selection ids, cache their article payloads, merge them back into the rendered list even when the query excludes them.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add web_search tool to article and feed chat
description: chat_with_article and chat_with_articles (Ask Skim) currently ground only on the local article text. Add a web_search tool the LLM can call when it decides the feed/article text is insufficient. Use an anthropic-compatible tool-use loop where the model can emit tool_use blocks invoking web_search. Backend wires to an existing search API (check commands/chat.rs::web_search; surface it to the provider). UI: citations list already exists — extend to include web sources distinctly. Works with claude-subscription and claude API providers; gracefully degrade on OpenAI/Ollama.
close_reason: Added Anthropic-style tool-use loop for chat_with_article and chat_with_articles. Extended ChatRequest with tools and ChatResponse with tool_uses/stop_reason. AnthropicProvider and ClaudeSubscriptionProvider send tools and parse tool_use blocks; OpenAI-compat, Ollama, local, and claude-cli gracefully drop tools and log. Tool-loop (max 3 iterations) gates on provider kind per spec. web_search command now delegates to run_web_search; both paths share implementation. Citations merged into ChatSource with source_type='web' plus a new WebCitation type on ChatResponse for per-article chat. UI updated: AskSkimDialog shows globe icon for web sources; ChatPanel renders web_citations under assistant bubbles. cargo check + tsc --noEmit green.

title: AI Inbox: user-supplied 'what I care about' prompt
description: Today the AI triage learns from engagement signals (reading time, stars, chat, summaries, priority overrides). Add an explicit user-supplied prompt that describes interests/filters in natural language. Surface in Settings; pass as part of the triage system prompt alongside the learned preference profile. UI: multi-line textarea under Settings > AI. Persist in AppSettings JSON (not a new DB column). Include in both triage_articles and generate_themes system prompts.
close_reason: Added AiSettings.triage_user_prompt free-form field. Threaded into triage and theme_grouping system prompts as '--- Reader's interests (explicit) ---' block, prepended before learned preferences. Settings UI exposes 5-row textarea under AI Provider.

title: Swift Tauri plugin: MLX + Foundation Models + iOS Keychain
description:
Port the Swift service code (previously in the now-deleted native ios/ SwiftUI dir — recoverable from git, commit 3dfc79f) into a Tauri iOS plugin at src-tauri/gen/apple/Sources/ (or a dedicated plugin crate).

Exposes these Tauri commands callable from React:
- mlx_is_available / mlx_is_model_downloaded / mlx_download_model(repoId) / mlx_complete({system, user, maxTokens, jsonMode}) — backed by mlx-swift (Qwen 2.5 3B default)
- fm_is_available / fm_complete(...) — Apple Foundation Models (iOS 26+), gated by #available
- ios_keychain_store(key, value) / ios_keychain_load(key) / ios_keychain_clear(key) — for OAuth token storage on iOS (desktop uses SQLite settings KV)

Reference implementations (from git history, native SwiftUI commit 3dfc79f):
- MLXRunner.swift + MLXModelManager.swift
- FoundationModelRunner.swift + FoundationModelSchemas.swift
- KeychainStore.swift

These already handle: thermal/background model eviction, iOS 26+ gating, @Generable structured triage output, 4-bit Qwen 2.5 3B download flow.

Blocked on: Xcode.app install (simulator build + Swift compile).

Acceptance: Tauri command from React returns MLX-generated triage JSON in iPhone 15 Pro simulator.
close_reason: Plugin scaffold landed under plugins/tauri-plugin-skim-ai/. Swift MLXRunner/FoundationModelRunner/KeychainStore ported from git history. Rust side exposes 10 mobile commands; desktop returns explicit unavailable errors. Plugin registered in main app + capabilities/default.json. Actual Swift compile + runtime test still blocked on Xcode.app install.

title: Pivot iOS/iPadOS to Tauri mobile (React + Rust shared with desktop)
description:
Decision: ship iOS as Tauri 2 mobile, not native SwiftUI. Reuse React frontend + Rust backend from desktop. Close native ios/ dir; port Swift service code (MLXRunner, FoundationModelRunner, ClaudeOAuth, KeychainStore) into a Tauri iOS plugin under src-tauri/gen/apple/.

Plan:
1. Run 'cargo tauri ios init' to scaffold gen/apple/
2. Feature-gate llama-cpp-2 out of iOS build (not cross-compilable); use MLX via Swift plugin for on-device LLM instead
3. Implement Claude OAuth directly in Rust (HTTP flow using reqwest) — replaces both desktop CLI subprocess path and ios Swift ClaudeOAuth.swift. Endpoints: claude.ai/oauth/authorize, console.anthropic.com/v1/oauth/token. client_id 9d1c250a-e61b-44d9-88ed-5944d1962f5e. Headers: Bearer + anthropic-beta: oauth-2025-04-20,claude-code-20250219. System prefix 'You are Claude Code...'
4. New Rust command(s): claude_oauth_begin / claude_oauth_exchange / claude_send_message
5. Swift Tauri plugin exposes: mlx_load / mlx_complete / fm_complete (iOS 26+)
6. React Settings page gains tier picker + sign-in UI. Desktop app uses same picker (consistency)
7. Drop ios/Skim/ SwiftUI dir (archive in a tag if wanted, but src is in git history)
8. Xcode install still required locally to build for simulator — unavoidable

Supersedes: skim-3ri, skim-4qd, skim-1oh, skim-i5i (all closed as native-ios landed, but practical work shifts to Tauri).
close_reason: Pivot structural work landed: Tauri iOS scaffold + Rust Claude OAuth + ClaudeSubscriptionProvider + React sign-in UI + native ios/ removed. Swift plugin port, lib.rs feature gating, and on-device tier UI are tracked as follow-ups.

title: iOS/iPadOS M2: MLX on-device model (Qwen 2.5 3B)
description:
Add MLX Swift runtime + Qwen 2.5 3B Instruct (4-bit) for offline triage and summary.

Approach:
- Add mlx-swift + mlx-swift-examples (MLXLMCommon, MLXLLM) via SwiftPM
- MLXRunner actor: lazy-load, stream tokens, evict on background + thermalState>=.serious
- Model download UI in Settings > On-device AI: pick model, URLSession background download, resumable, progress bar
- Store under Application Support/Models, isExcludedFromBackup=true
- Route triage + single-article summary to on-device when selected

Target devices:
- iPhone 15 Pro+ / iPad M1+ for 3B quantized (~2GB)
- Fallback to cloud Claude for older devices

Acceptance:
- Triage batch of 20 articles returns valid JSON {id,priority,reason} on iPhone 15 Pro simulator
- 30-word summaries generated on-device
- Model evicts when backgrounded
- Works iPhone + iPad simulators on macbook (note: simulator uses host Metal, so quality/speed differs from real device — test on real hardware before ship)
close_reason: M2 MLX runtime + Qwen 2.5 3B implemented

title: iOS/iPadOS M1: Claude OAuth subscription auth
description:
Implement Claude Pro/Max OAuth flow on iOS/iPadOS so user can use subscription without API key and without claude -p CLI (neither works on iOS).

Approach:
- ASWebAuthenticationSession + PKCE against claude.ai OAuth endpoint
- Token format sk-ant-oat01_*, stored in Keychain with iCloud-sync access group (share across user's Apple devices)
- Call POST https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages with:
  - Authorization: Bearer <token>
  - anthropic-beta: oauth-2025-04-20
  - anthropic-version: 2023-06-01
- Refresh-token handling, token rotation, revocation fallback
- Replace placeholder anthropicCompletion in ios/Skim/Sources/Services/AIService.swift:149

Acceptance:
- User taps Sign in with Claude, browser OAuth completes, returns to app, triage+summary+chat all work
- Works in iPhone + iPad simulators on macbook
- Token persists across app launches
- No API key UX required

Risk: undocumented Anthropic OAuth path, ToS uncertainty. Keep behind provider enum so swappable.
close_reason: M1 OAuth flow, Keychain, Settings UI, SignIn view, and provider extension landed. Wire-up to existing call sites tracked as follow-up.

title: Replace regex smart folders with LLM-based grouping
description: User feedback: regex-based smart folder UI is terrible UX and they want LLM grouping instead. Replace the current rules builder with either (a) a plain-English description the LLM matches feeds against, or (b) one-shot auto-organize that clusters all feeds into folders.
close_reason: Replaced regex smart folder UI with two AI flows: auto-organize (clusters all feeds) and description-based smart folder (LLM matches feeds to a topic). Committed 7420bcb.

title: Default listFilter to unread
description: All Articles view defaulted to all; user wants unread by default.
close_reason: Closed

title: Strengthen selected-article highlight in ArticleList
description: Current selected style is bg-white/10 which is nearly invisible in screenshot. Add stronger visual cue (left accent bar + brighter background).
close_reason: Closed

title: Rewrite README with features + screenshots
description: Replace the stub Tauri template README. Add sections: what Skim is, key features (3-pane + AI Inbox, auto-organize, Ask Skim, catch-up, summarize, engagement learning, on-device AI via MLX/Apple Intelligence, Claude Pro/Max via OAuth), screenshots from docs/screenshots/, build/dev/install instructions.
close_reason: README rewritten with feature list + screenshots.

title: Create SPEC.md documenting all features
description: Write docs/SPEC.md that catalogs every feature currently shipping: feed ingestion (RSS/Atom/OPML/Feedly OAuth), folders (manual + smart folders with regex rules), AI Inbox triage (priority 1-5 + reason), Themes, Super-quick catch-up, Ask Skim chat over feed, per-article chat, summarization (length/tone/custom prompt), auto-organize with AI, engagement tracking, local/cloud/claude-subscription AI providers, MLX on-device tier (iOS), Apple Foundation Models tier (iOS 26+), OAuth subscription auth (claude.ai + feedly). Group by subsystem. Link each feature to the relevant screenshot in docs/screenshots/.
close_reason: docs/SPEC.md written, covers all 17 subsystems.

title: Fix auto-organize dialog scrollbar (top-right visual glitch)
description: After running Auto-organize with AI analysis, the scrollbar in the top-right of the proposals panel looks weird (see docs/screenshots/03-auto-organize.png). Investigate the scrollbar styling or container overflow and fix.
close_reason: Restructured AutoOrganizeDialog to flex column: fixed header, scrolling proposals list (flex-1 min-h-0 overflow-y-auto), fixed footer. Added overflow-hidden to outer rounded-2xl container so webkit scrollbar clips inside the rounded corners.

title: Feature-gate lib.rs + provider.rs for iOS target compile
description:
Make src-tauri compile for aarch64-apple-ios(-sim) by gating the llama-cpp-2 paths:
- lib.rs: use ai::local_provider::* imports + model_state + preload thread
- provider.rs: the 'local' match arm that constructs LocalLlmProvider
- Wrap in #[cfg(not(target_os = "ios"))] and define a stub SharedModelState type alias on iOS so existing command signatures compile.

Currently llama-cpp-2 is feature-gated out in Cargo.toml, but the code that references local_provider::* still fails iOS build.

Blocked on: Xcode.app install to actually run 'cargo tauri ios build'.
close_reason: Feature-gating applied: ai/mod.rs conditionally loads local_provider_ios stub; provider.rs 'local' arm gated; lib.rs preload+eviction wrapped in #[cfg(not(target_os="ios"))]. Desktop build verified green. Full iOS target compile still needs Xcode SDK.

title: React Settings: on-device AI tier picker (MLX, Apple Intelligence)
description:
Once the Swift Tauri plugin (see related bead) ships, extend SettingsDialog with:
- 'On-device' option (route auto-picks Foundation Models when available, else MLX)
- MLX model picker + download progress UI (invokes mlx_download_model)
- Apple Intelligence availability indicator
- Per-task routing preview (triage/summary → on-device; chat/themes → cloud)

Mirrors what the now-deleted native ios/ Settings had. Desktop also benefits — users can run triage locally on Mac via MLX.
close_reason: Added on-device tier picker (MLX + Apple Intelligence) to SettingsDialog with graceful fallbacks for not-yet-registered plugin.

title: MLX: validate triage JSON quality on Qwen 2.5 3B
description: Run sample triage of 20 RSS articles on real iPhone 15 Pro; verify JSON schema compliance and priority calibration vs Claude baseline. Track failure modes.

title: Wire ModelRouter into triage/summary/chat call sites
description:
M1 landed the OAuth/provider surface + Settings UI + ModelRouter. Remaining glue: existing call sites that invoke AIService.triageArticles / summarizeArticle currently pass a provider literal. Update them to consult ModelRouter.shared.tier(for:) to pick the right tier dynamically (claudeSubscription when signed in, mlx/foundationModels on-device, api-key fallback).

Call sites to update (search for AIService invocations under ios/Skim/Sources). Also wire ClaudeOAuth token retrieval for the claudeSubscription path and Keychain anthropicApiKey for the fallback.

title: iOS/iPadOS M6: iPad-optimized layout + polish
description:
Ship proper iPad 3-pane UX and iPhone adaptive layout.

Scope:
- NavigationSplitView: Sidebar (folders/feeds) | Article list | Detail. Already skeleton in ios/Skim/Sources/Views/ContentView.swift
- iPad: 3-column always visible in landscape, sidebar-collapses in portrait
- iPhone: 2-pane stack, detail pushes
- Keyboard shortcuts: j/k next/prev, r mark read, s star, / search, cmd-n add feed
- Drag-drop OPML import on iPad
- Pull-to-refresh
- Swipe actions: mark read/unread, star, triage-priority-override
- Dynamic type + VoiceOver pass

Acceptance:
- Works iPhone + iPad simulators on macbook
- No layout breaks between portrait/landscape
- Keyboard shortcuts work on iPad with hardware keyboard
- OPML drag-drop import succeeds
close_reason: M6 layout polish complete: keyboard shortcuts, swipe actions, pull-to-refresh, OPML drop target, adaptive column visibility

title: iOS/iPadOS M3: Apple Foundation Models tier (iOS 26+)
description:
Add Foundation Models framework as preferred on-device tier when available.

Approach:
- import FoundationModels, LanguageModelSession
- Gate with #available(iOS 26, *) + SystemLanguageModel.default.isAvailable (Apple Intelligence device check)
- Guided generation via @Generable structs — typed JSON for triage schema {id, priority, reason}
- FoundationModelRunner actor parallel to MLXRunner
- ModelRouter picks: FoundationModels (if available) > MLX (if downloaded) > Claude OAuth > API key

Constraints:
- ~4K context, no streaming tool-use yet
- Apple-managed model, zero download, zero config
- Only Apple Intelligence devices (iPhone 15 Pro+, iPad M1+)

Acceptance:
- Triage works via FoundationModels on supported simulator
- Falls through gracefully on unsupported devices
- Router choice visible in Settings
close_reason: M3 Foundation Models tier implemented (iOS 26+ gated)

title: Phase 2: flat feed folders
description: Manual folders (flat, no nesting) to group feeds in sidebar. New folders table (id, name, sort_order). feeds.folder_id column. CRUD commands. Drag-drop + context menu to assign feed to folder.
close_reason: Phases 2+3 shipped: manual folders + smart folders with regex/URL/OPML-category rules

title: Phase 1: rename + remove feed
description: Right-click context menu on sidebar feed with Rename and Remove. Rename updates feeds.title. Remove deletes feed cascade; if any articles are starred, prompt user with count before deleting.
close_reason: Phase 1 shipped: rename inline + remove with starred-count confirm

title: Feedly OAuth login flow
description: Replace paste-token UX with OAuth login: localhost redirect listener, browser-launched authorization, token exchange, refresh-token handling. Settings stores client_id/secret.
close_reason: OAuth flow implemented; awaiting Feedly cred approval to bake in releases

title: Feedly sync: connect/disconnect commands + settings UI
description: Add connect_feedly, disconnect_feedly, get_feedly_status commands. Add Feedly connection section to Sync tab in SettingsDialog.
close_reason: Closed

title: Feedly sync: bidirectional read/star state sync
description: Push read/star state changes to Feedly via tokio::spawn fire-and-forget.
close_reason: Closed

title: Feedly sync: Feedly-aware feed refresh
description: Modify refresh_feed and refresh_all_feeds to use Feedly streams API for feeds with feedly_id. Update import_feedly to use stream fetcher.
close_reason: Closed

title: Feedly sync: expand API client with streams, markers, tags
description: Add verify_token, fetch_stream_contents, feedly_entries_to_articles, mark_entries_read/unread, save/unsave_entry to feedly.rs
close_reason: Closed

title: Feedly sync: add feedly_entry_id to articles schema
description: Add feedly_entry_id column to articles table, update Article model and all queries. Foundation for Feedly bidirectional sync.
close_reason: Closed

title: MLX: resumable model download + background URLSession
description: Current download may not survive app suspension. Switch to URLSessionConfiguration.background if hub loader doesn't handle this natively.

title: FM: wire guided-generation triage path in AIService
description: Once FoundationModelRunner.triageStructured is stable, bypass string JSON parsing in AIService.triageArticles for the .foundationModels provider. Expected reliability improvement on older devices and smaller batches.

title: Parse dropped OPML and import feeds on iOS
description: The iPad drag-drop target in ContentView currently just logs dropped URLs. Wire it to a proper OPML parser that extracts feed URLs and imports them via FeedService.fetchAndParseFeed + AppState.addFeed for each feed. Consider using a lightweight XMLParser-based routine or a package like OPMLKit.

title: [DEFERRED] iOS/iPadOS M5: CloudKit sync with desktop
description:
DEFERRED — not in initial scope. File for future work.

Mirror core tables to CloudKit private DB so iOS/iPadOS and macOS Tauri stay in sync:
- feeds
- articles.is_read, is_starred
- article_triage
- folders (manual + smart rules)
- article_interactions

Conflict resolution: record modification date wins. Free (CloudKit private tier), Apple-native.

Blocked-by: M1, M2, M6 ship first. Revisit after standalone iOS is usable.

title: Phase 3: smart folders
description: Rule-based auto-populated folders. folders.is_smart + rules_json. v1 rules: regex on feed title/url, OPML category match. AI classification in v2.
close_reason: Phases 2+3 shipped: manual folders + smart folders with regex/URL/OPML-category rules

title: [PROBABLY WON'T DO] iOS Mac-bridge fallback for Claude subscription
description:
PROBABLY WON'T DO — recorded for future reference only.

Idea: if OAuth subscription path (M1) becomes unreliable (Anthropic revokes, ToS change, refresh breakage), fall back to bridging Claude subscription from the Mac Tauri app over LAN.

Design sketch:
- Tauri desktop exposes small HTTP/WebSocket server on 127.0.0.1:PORT + advertises via Bonjour _skim._tcp
- iOS discovers via NetService, pairs via QR code (Mac shows → iOS scans → shared secret stored in Keychain)
- iOS sends chat/triage requests → Mac proxies to its claude-cli provider (src-tauri/src/ai/provider.rs ClaudeCliProvider)
- Remote access over Tailscale MagicDNS or CloudKit relay — no public exposure
- Mac must be awake for it to work

Why probably won't do:
- OAuth path (M1) should be sufficient
- Adds significant complexity on both sides (Rust server + iOS discovery + pairing UX)
- Degrades UX: iOS only works when Mac is online

Revisit trigger: OAuth flow blocked by Anthropic or proves unreliable in practice.

value: Feedly OAuth: redirect URI is http://127.0.0.1:54321/feedly/callback (hardcoded port). Client_id/secret baked at build via FEEDLY_CLIENT_ID/FEEDLY_CLIENT_SECRET env vars using option_env!; falls back to user-entered creds in Settings. Feedly requires emailing partners@feedly.com to get creds, must obfuscate in binaries per their policy.

Feed folders: flat structure, folders table (id, name, sort_order, is_smart, rules_json). feeds has folder_id + opml_category. Smart folder rules are JSON {mode: any|all, rules: [{type: regex_title|regex_url|opml_category, pattern|value}]}. Evaluated both backend (list_folders returns feed_count) and frontend (src/lib/smartFolder.ts).
